name: agent-team-framework
description: Reusable framework for managing multi-agent coding teams with clear roles, shared knowledge, quality gates, deployment flow, and governance.
when: Use when setting up or auditing a project that needs coordinated AI coding agents.

name: ai-ppt-generator
description: Generate branded presentation decks from structured prompts, brand assets, and slide assembly scripts.
when: Use when creating a new deck with a repeatable brand-driven slide workflow.

name: canva
description: Manage Canva designs, assets, exports, and folders through the Canva Connect API.
when: Use when automating Canva asset management, export, or design organization.

name: caveman
description: Simplify complex concepts into clear, accessible explanations for non-technical audiences.
when: Use for ELI5-style explanations, simplification, or audience-friendly rewrites.

name: coding-discipline
description: Applies disciplined coding rules before, during, and after code changes: think first, keep changes surgical, verify before done.
when: Use before any code change, refactor, feature, deploy, or bug fix.

name: content-strategy
description: Build content strategy, pillars, calendars, distribution plans, repurposing workflows, and performance metrics.
when: Use when planning what content to create, where to distribute it, and how to measure it.

name: council-plus
description: Runs a full Guru Council workflow from framing to council synthesis, stress-testing, PRD, issues, and handoff.
when: Use when a topic deserves complete strategic review and execution packaging.

name: diagnose
description: Structured troubleshooting process for identifying root causes behind errors, failures, or unexpected behavior.
when: Use when something is broken, unclear, failing, or needs systematic debugging.

name: frontend-performance
description: Analyze and improve frontend performance across Core Web Vitals, loading, bundle size, stability, and runtime efficiency.
when: Use when pages feel slow, bundles are heavy, or Core Web Vitals need improvement.

name: grill-me
description: Relentlessly interviews and stress-tests plans, strategies, or designs until assumptions and dependencies are clear.
when: Use when you want a plan challenged, interrogated, or pressure-tested.

name: grill-with-docs
description: Stress-tests plans or interface designs against domain documentation and updates docs inline.
when: Use when specs, terminology, or architecture must stay aligned with a domain model.

name: handoff
description: Compacts current work into a clear handoff document for another agent or future session.
when: Use when work needs to continue elsewhere without losing context.

name: improve-codebase-architecture
description: Finds architecture deepening opportunities: shallow modules, tight coupling, untestable code, and AI-navigation issues.
when: Use when auditing or planning refactors for maintainability and testability.

name: keyword-research
description: SEO keyword research and topic discovery with intent classification, clustering, prioritization, and report templates.
when: Use when planning search-led content, topic clusters, or keyword strategy.

name: linkedin-cli
description: CLI utilities for LinkedIn profile search, feed summaries, message checks, and session-based workflows.
when: Use when automating LinkedIn scouting or lightweight relationship intelligence.

name: mattpocock-skills
description: Collection index of reusable engineering and thinking skills adapted from Matt Pocock-style agent workflows.
when: Use as a gateway/reference for related skills such as TDD, handoff, PRD, triage, and grilling.

name: ontology
description: Typed knowledge graph pattern for structured memory, entities, relationships, constraints, and cross-skill state.
when: Use when information needs to be linked, queried, validated, or shared across skills.

name: prototype
description: Build throwaway prototypes to explore interfaces, APIs, state models, or product ideas before committing to production code.
when: Use when testing possible approaches quickly and cheaply.

name: react-expert
description: React 18+ / 19 guidance for component architecture, hooks, state, Server Components, Suspense, performance, and testing.
when: Use when building or reviewing modern React applications.

name: self-improvement
description: Captures mistakes, corrections, failures, feature requests, and reusable lessons for continuous agent improvement.
when: Use after failures, user corrections, tool/API issues, or recurring workflow discoveries.

name: ssot-knowledge-graph
description: Connects SSOT files to projects, trackers, status docs, content indexes, and memory pointers.
when: Use when new knowledge needs to be classified, cross-referenced, and connected.

name: ssot-setting
description: Snapshots, backs up, and restores OpenClaw configuration as an SSOT-controlled workflow.
when: Use before/after config changes or when restoring a known-good configuration state.

name: ssot-steward
description: Enforces SSOT file naming, versioning, archive, upload, and branded-PDF workflow rules.
when: Use for Google Drive / Good Steward file operations, version bumps, uploads, and PDF generation.

name: Stock Images
description: Finds free stock photos and placeholder image sources using direct URL patterns and provider guidance.
when: Use when sourcing visual placeholders or royalty-free stock imagery.

name: tdd
description: Test-driven development workflow using red-green-refactor and behavior-first implementation.
when: Use when building features or fixing bugs test-first.

name: to-issues
description: Turns plans, PRDs, or conversations into prioritized, independently completable issue tickets.
when: Use when a strategy or spec needs actionable implementation tasks.

name: to-prd
description: Synthesizes conversation context into a structured Product Requirements Document.
when: Use when requirements need to be documented as a PRD/spec.

name: triage
description: Sorts incoming messages, files, emails, or ideas into prioritized action buckets.
when: Use when processing a batch of inputs or deciding what matters first.

name: ui-audit
description: Audits UI against UX principles: hierarchy, accessibility, cognition, navigation, feedback, and usability patterns.
when: Use when reviewing an interface or design for UX quality.

name: ui-ux-pro-max
description: Searchable UI/UX design databases and scripts for styles, palettes, typography, charts, stacks, and design systems.
when: Use when generating design direction or choosing UI patterns from structured references.

name: weather
description: Gets current weather and forecasts from no-key public weather providers.
when: Use when a task needs quick weather context.

name: website-seo
description: Universal on-page SEO system covering page optimization, schema, technical checks, internal links, Core Web Vitals, and gaps.
when: Use when optimizing websites, pages, or content for search visibility.

name: zoom-out
description: Steps back from details to identify larger patterns, strategic connections, assumptions, and implications.
when: Use when you need big-picture perspective or “what am I missing?” analysis.
